results.title: The ONLY Chess Guide you need (1 hour+)
results.reason: The 80-minute duration and comprehensive timestamps indicate a deep dive that is highly valuable for serious intermediate study.
summary: Learn how every chess piece moves and works
Master the basic rules to start playing games
Understand how to control the center of the board
Discover simple ways to attack and defend pieces

results.title: 35 Vital Chess Principles | Opening, Middlegame, and Endgame Principles - Chess Strategy and Ideas
results.reason: Chess Vibes is known for structured, principle-based learning that bridges the gap between beginner and intermediate levels effectively.
summary: Follow smart rules for the start of the game
Learn how to improve your pieces in the middle
Understand how to win when few pieces remain
Build a solid plan for every stage of play

results.title: 7 MOST COMMON Chess Mistakes
results.reason: Excellent for intermediates; it uses a long-form format to explain the 'why' behind common mistakes, which is superior for actual improvement.
summary: Identify the most frequent errors that lose games
Learn how to stop giving away free pieces
Understand why moving the same piece twice hurts
Discover how to keep your king safe from danger

results.title: Basic Chess Openings Explained
results.reason: GothamChess has massive reach and high viewer appreciation for making complex opening concepts accessible and entertaining.
summary: Learn the best ways to start a chess match
Understand why controlling the middle is very important
See how to develop your pieces quickly and safely
Get familiar with the most popular opening moves

results.title: Fischer's 3 Rules to Prevent 90% of Your Chess Mistakes
results.reason: Remote Chess Academy uses classic principles (Fischer's rules) to provide a clear, actionable framework for reducing errors.
summary: Apply three simple rules to avoid big blunders
Learn to check for threats before every single move
Understand how to keep your pieces working together
Improve your focus to stop making easy mistakes
